body:
Fixes #8

## Summary

- Followed up on the successful `v0.9.4` release and found the remaining broken desktop release contract: the README and Pages site link to stable Linux `linux-x64` assets, but Electron Builder uploaded `vk-bot-desktop-linux-x86_64.AppImage` and `vk-bot-desktop-linux-amd64.deb`.
- Pins the Linux AppImage, Debian package, and tarball artifact names to the documented `linux-x64` filenames.
- Adds release workflow validation so Linux/Windows stable assets, `SHA256SUMS.txt`, and `BUILD-PROVENANCE.txt` must exist before `gh release upload` runs.
- Adds a regression test and a patch changeset so the next merge creates a corrected release.
- Extends `docs/case-studies/issue-8` with the `v0.9.4` run, release assets, direct link checks, and the full post-PR9 Electron release log.

## Root Cause

PR 9 restored release publishing and produced `v0.9.4`, but the successful release showed that `${arch}` is not a stable public filename contract for every Linux target: AppImage expanded to `x86_64`, Debian expanded to `amd64`, and the documented `linux-x64` latest-download URLs returned 404.

macOS artifacts remain intentionally skipped until the signing/notarization secrets are configured; this PR keeps that policy unchanged and focuses on the Linux stable-link mismatch.

## Verification

- `npm test`
- `npm run check`
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh`
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `GITHUB_BASE_REF=main node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `npx --yes -p secretlint -p @secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend secretlint "**/*"`
- `npm run build:renderer`
- `npm run build:site`
- `npm install --no-save --package-lock=false --no-audit --no-fund browser-commander@0.8.0 playwright@1.59.1`
- `npx playwright install chromium`
- `npm run test:pages:e2e -- --site-dir site/dist`
- `npm run electron:build:linux`
- Local Linux artifact smoke check confirmed `vk-bot-desktop-linux-x64.AppImage`, `vk-bot-desktop-linux-x64.deb`, and `vk-bot-desktop-linux-x64.tar.gz` exist and are inspectable.
title: Fix stable Linux release asset names
